name: Vue.js Patterns & Best Practices
description: Comprehensive guide to Vue.js component patterns, Composition API, state management, and performance optimization
version_note: Patterns focus on Vue 3 with Composition API, with notes for Options API where relevant

composition_api.description: Modern Vue 3 API for organizing component logic

setup_function.example:
<script setup>
import { ref, computed, onMounted } from 'vue'

const count = ref(0)
const doubleCount = computed(() => count.value * 2)

function increment() {
  count.value++
}

onMounted(() => {
  console.log('Component mounted')
})
</script>

ref.example:
const count = ref(0)
console.log(count.value) // access with .value

reactive.example:
const state = reactive({ count: 0, name: 'John' })
console.log(state.count) // no .value needed

readonly.example:
const state = reactive({ count: 0 })
const readonlyState = readonly(state)

toRefs.use: Convert reactive object to refs for destructuring
toRefs.example:
const state = reactive({ count: 0, name: 'John' })
const { count, name } = toRefs(state)

watchEffect.when: Don't need old value, automatic tracking

provide_inject.use_when: Pass data deep without props drilling

teleport.description: Render component content elsewhere in DOM
teleport.use_when: Modals, toasts, tooltips that need to escape parent overflow

pinia.usage:
import { useCounterStore } from '@/stores/counter'

const counter = useCounterStore()
counter.increment()
console.log(counter.count)

vuex.note: Use Pinia for new Vue 3 projects
vuex.migration: Pinia is easier and more flexible

v_memo.when: Heavy computations that depend on specific values

shallowRef_shallowReactive.use_when: Large data structures, only top level changes
shallowRef_shallowReactive.example:
const state = shallowReactive({ nested: { deep: { value: 1 } } })
// Only state.nested triggers updates, not deep mutations

typescript.refs:
const count = ref<number>(0)
const input = ref<HTMLInputElement | null>(null)

vitest.description: Fast unit test framework for Vite projects

best_practices: Use Composition API for new Vue 3 projects
Prefer `<script setup>` syntax for cleaner code
Use ref for primitives, reactive for objects
Use Pinia for state management (not Vuex)
Use computed for derived state (not methods)
Use slots for flexible component composition
Lazy load routes and heavy components
Provide meaningful key attributes in v-for
Follow Vue style guide naming conventions
Test components with Vue Test Utils + Vitest
anti_patterns: Using v-if with v-for on same element
Not providing keys in v-for lists
Not using TypeScript in new projects
Using Options API for new Vue 3 projects
Mixing Options API and Composition API
Not using Pinia (using Vuex for Vue 3)

breaking_changes: Global API changes (Vue.createApp instead of new Vue)
